design_plan: rng=42; AIDA: hero=attention, cache thesis=interest, Gemini facts=desire, CTA=action; hero width targets 2–3 lines at 1120px; bento grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense verified; all competitors label sweep complete; primary CTA contrast checked (ink on paper).
headline: Super vs Gemini — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Gemini. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Gemini

summary: Gemini is rapidly pushing computer‑use agents into its ecosystem, starting with Gemini 3.5 Flash in public preview. Super is built specifically for people who want a personal AI agent that actually operates a computer — and reuses a computer-use cache so repeated workflows get faster and cheaper over time.
